title: Drive and Talent
journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
abstract: Journal Article Drive and Talent Get access Botond Kőszegi, Botond Kőszegi 1University of California, Berkeley Search for other works by this author on: Oxford Academic Google Scholar Wei Li Wei Li 2University of California, Riverside Search for other works by this author on: Oxford Academic Google Scholar Journal of the European Economic Association, Volume 6, Issue 1, 1 March 2008, Pages 210–236, https://doi.org/10.1162/JEEA.2008.6.1.210 Published: 01 March 2008
summary_en: Why do some talented people underperform and less-talented ones overperform? This paper models the interaction between "drive" (effort motivation) and "talent" (ability). When talent is uncertain, feedback-inducing investments reveal it, and agents with different motivational structures react differently. The result: drive and talent act as substitutes in some settings but complements in others. The paper helps explain when self-selection of the driven into demanding jobs leads to positive or negative correlation with observed ability. A contribution to the economics of career choice and motivation.
policy_relevance: For Hungarian career-counselling policy, talent-management HR practice, and public-sector hiring. The paper highlights that selection based on past achievement aggregates drive and talent in complex ways; simple meritocratic rules can have unintended effects. Caveat: theoretical and somewhat abstract; operational Hungarian applications require empirical measurement of the drive-talent correlation in specific occupations.
